q: Which correctly describes spermatogenesis vs. oogenesis in terms of cell products?
options: Spermatogenesis produces one functional sperm per primary spermatocyte; oogenesis produces four egg cells per primary oocyte
Both processes produce four equal haploid cells per meiotic division
Spermatogenesis produces four functional spermatids per primary spermatocyte; oogenesis produces one large secondary oocyte and smaller polar bodies that degenerate
Oogenesis is complete at birth; spermatogenesis begins at puberty and ends at death
explanation: Spermatogenesis yields four equal functional sperm; oogenesis divides cytoplasm unequally to produce one large egg (retaining most cytoplasm/nutrients) and non-functional polar bodies.
